At closing, however, the numbers did not match what I had been told earlier, and I was asked to bring additional money to closing to buy down the rate, which had not been clearly explained up front.

Because of these issues, I decided not to proceed and I would caution others to be very careful, double?check all documents, and confirm every number in writing before committing. The mostly have junior staff and overseas support staff -- who either don't respond or make errors in the evaluation of your underwriting.   The whole process drags on and on and you are left in limbo.   Requests to speak to supervisors and others with experience go unaddressed.

My housing association has a lawsuit again it that is in the process of being resolved -- with a settlement in the near future.

This was especially egregious when my most important asset was on the line and was treated like it was practically nothing. review_text:
I got a HELOC through Better in August 2024. It wasn't a wonderful rate but overall the actual process of getting the HELOC was not terrible. When I asked about 'draws' and how that would work, they cheerfully said 'oh we'll send you checks and it's really easy' (after the 90 day lockout period).
   But here is the basic problem with Better.. Once you close, you don't mean anything to them and they almost immediately will transfer you to a servicer like Shellpoint.. who to put it mildly are a nightmare. To give you an idea.. Shellpoint don't even mention HELOCS on their servicing app, let alone give you information on ordering checks etc. This is something you 100% have to drive with them and it is an absolute horrible nightmare. For example, if they state they will send them, you don't get any any way to track the request, record the request, expedite the request and timeline? "30 days or more"  and there is nothing you can do if the request goes nowhere..  If there is one piece of advice I would beg folks out there to listen to is to avoid getting a HELOC from anyone that either doesn't service it on the backend or basically doesn't care about you after closing. There are for sure 'Better' options than better and your first stop should be to a credit union.. In our family, we will never use Better for any product again! We have learned our lesson.

N.B There should be a reply option here. But an empty corporate response like this one is frankly part of the problem. It's disingenuous because I have already 'discussed my experience' - you can read it right here. I'm not naive.. it's business and your business is all about selling on the frontend.. you can always do the stock 'Golly, we're sorry things didn't go well for you..' to try and damage control, and then move on as in this case. But.. If you genuinely cared on the other hand, there are many things you could do e.g 'We will review the provider and commit to no longer working with them if data supports the assertions.. ' and/or 'We'll work with our servicers and make sure that all servicers clearly offer details on accessing the HELOC and processing draws.. we will define some minimum standards such as x and servicers that don't meet those standards will not be people we work with.. etc etc'. See the difference.. the latter is the type of quite reasonable response that a business that truly cares about the post-sales experience would issue.. very different to the empty corporate response here. If you want to be 'Better' then mean it.. show it. Just once.. go beyond the empty contrived and frankly meaningless responses and let people know what you did specifically to try and improve this in the future. That will actually end up winning you customers.. Try it..

review_text: I was given a rate over the phone and "rate locked" that rate - only to have it raised just a week prior to closing. On 10/31 I stopped shopping other lenders due to my loan officer getting me a great rate at Better. The day I "locked" I was very clear that we were purchasing a condo - my loan consultant hadn't realized this and said that may change the rate. He put it into the system then came back with good news - the rate actually went down! Unfortunately, this was too good to be true - two weeks later my rate suddenly adjusted up so I would owe $4000 more at closing because they hadn't realized it was a condo, even though I had a very clear conversation saying it was and was given that rate with that understanding from my loan consultant. He apologized for the error, but said there was absolutely nothing he could do, even though this was their mistake. So now, due to an error Better made, I owe either $4000 more at closing or $150 more per month that I was "rate locked" at, and I no longer can shop with other lenders as I am too close to closing.

review_text:
This company is incredibly disappointing.

Following my financial advisor?s recommendation, I applied for a $150K HELOC. As a stay-at-home father (my wife passed away), I rely on life insurance proceeds for income. I explained my situation to their advisor, provided all the necessary information, and was told we could proceed.

The next day, a sales manager named Daniel called, rudely questioning why I wasn?t moving the loan along. I had completed every step as outlined on their portal, but they rejected some documents. When I spoke with Helen, a loan coordinator, she said I uploaded the wrong files. I pointed out that I followed their website?s instructions and explained that some documents kept disappearing from the portal. She didn?t seem to care about the technical issues I was facing. After re-uploading everything, I received confirmation that the title search and credit check were moving forward.

Then, despite having completed all tasks, I got an email saying they wouldn?t approve the $150K line because my son?s SSA benefits only last for three years. I had already provided proof of my financials, showing I had four times the line amount in one account, a near-800 credit score, and only $30K left on my mortgage. I even suggested reducing the line to align with my monthly draw amounts.

Instead, they came back offering just $50K. They had all the necessary information from the start to make this decision.

In my opinion, they?re neither upfront nor knowledgeable, and I wouldn?t trust them. It feels like a bait-and-switch: promise $150K, then offer only $50K.

I pulled my application and would recommend others look elsewhere. I?m more frustrated by the time I wasted with them.

The second I locked my rate, I could never get anyone on the phone. It went from full availability to work through things to maybe one time slot open a week and a half out
Even when booking those times slots I would get the call from better 20 minutes late!
Better also could not seem to understand due dates, which for a mortgage company is a massive deal. Any time something was due, whether it be a mortgage commitment or even the closing itself, Better showed no sense of urgency. The only way I got any documents from them was cold calling thier public line over and over until someone would finally look into what was going on.
To top it all off, Better almost completely screwed up my closing costing me the house.
Less than 24 hours before hitting the closing table, I called better to make sure everything was still set. Of course, when I get them, my cash to close had gone up 20% from the night before. Less than 24 hours before closing...I spent the better part of that day on the phone and on hold with Better trying to get an answer on why the cash to close went up so steeply, and also why it happened less than 24 hours before closing....
After hours, literally hours, of pain staking calls, being passed from person to person, they finally had their mistake pointed out by my title company (who I would recommend to anyone! Carriage House Title).
Had someone from my title company not taken the time to go through line by line with better, this would never have been caught.

I cannot tell you to stay away enough. This review barely touched the issues I had with better.
The experience was absolutely horrendous.
The incompetence shown by this company is downright astounding.
Learn from my mistake, just go through your local credit union. Better is certainly anything but what they claim to be. They were the worst financial experience I have ever had

review_text:
Better Mortgage messed up my 1st loan application.  I was preapproved, but then they denied my loan on the last day of the financial contingency. I already had everything ready to go, utilities set up, furniture and appliances scheduled to ship. And I had to cancel all that. They should never had let me go that far and denied my loan at the end.  It let down so many parties.
I asked them how much I could qualify if I could not qualify for the original amount. They had me filled out a 2nd application. On the 2nd application, they verified that I should have qualified for the original loan amount. So I had to go through the process all over again. This time the seller didn't trust me because I canceled the contract on the last day of the financial contingency. I had to put down non-refundable deposit for the seller to sell me the house.  Anyway, they did approve the same loan on my 2nd application. I was able to buy the house eventually.  But due to the mistake they made on my first loan application, I now have a higher interest rate (because mortgage rates have gone up since then), and I spent so much time on canceling all the deliveries and getting refunds on them.

We financed through Better. Things started off well, but the day before closing they stated we would have an excess of credits. We decided to use that excess to buy down points and we also agreed to remove one of our insurance fees this would have covered it.

On the day of closing the numbers changed and I had to bring more money to the closing table.

It was very difficult to get a hold of anyone who was licensed to speak about rates. When we finally did get someone 2-3 hours before the actual signing there was an oversight on the numbers they provided us. We could not roll things back unless we waited another three business days.

TLDR; In the end we had to buy down points, which we did not want to do. It costs us an optional insurance and bringing additional money to closing. To recoup just the cash portion brought in it would take 2 years. Very bad transaction.

review_text:
Overall, I did end up closing with Better, but I felt like my mortgage coordinator and loan officer acted slowly and without transparency. I read negative reviews about Better and ignored them and really wish I hadn't. 

When I asked for reasons why closing took longer than expected or what documents they were waiting on, I was frequently met with shrugs or opaque "we need the documents." I would have closed much faster and with less hassle if I had gone with a credit union and I wish I had shopped around more. I felt like I was in the dark for most of the process, and the staff were unclear about what documents they needed to close.

The only real benefit of Better is that they ordered an appraisal on the early side and they budged a little when I showed them a better rate from UN Federal Credit Union. Lots of other options offer rate comparisons and online options to identify rates so SHOP AROUND EARLY!

Overall, it wasn't a nightmare, but I wouldn't choose them, and would not recommend them to anyone.

review_text: Using Better Mortgage for our HELOC was the worst financial decision I've ever made.  I chose them for a number of reasons, including what I perceived to be a fast, efficient online process.  Nothing could be further from the truth.  My HELOC application began February 13th and it FINALLY closed May 3rd.  The over documentation they required (not to mention the duplication) was a nightmare.  There were delays on every turn.  Had TWO major issues with title work, that I had to research and solve myself.  I did research on many HELOCs and almost all had no closing costs if the account remained open for 36 months.  That's what I though was the case here as well.  I was dead wrong and I should have been sure, but I believe Better Mortgage "hid" this in the documentation I was inundated with.  It was a $12,000.00 mistake that they made off of me for an absolute nightmare of a process, where I did most of the leg work.  I was fleeced, no doubt.

review_text:
Good company. Their staff was nice and mostly knowledgeable. My only issue was that I had to pay $600 for an appraisal just to compare lenders. I went with the other lender and couldn?t figure out how to get my $600 refunded.  A year later I finally filed a complaint with BBB and they quickly issued a refund. Highly unusual that you would need a refund and despite this, I highly recommend using Better.com. 

As an underwriter, I am intricately familiar with the lending process. There were several things I was required to provide that were to check a box rather than a true need. This is where I didn?t feel their staff understood enough of underwriting to be able to leave off the non-essentials that add no value and waste your time. They have a team that makes sure all boxes are checked BEFORE you go to underwriters?¦and those initial people don?t underwrite, so they have no concept when you try to explain why that particular piece of info is irrelevant and not required by law.  And I confirmed that these items weren?t ??required? because the other lender (that ended up closing my loan) never asked for them. 

All in all, the rate I got from Better was umm?¦slightly better.  I went with the other lender because they seemed more experienced and mostly, he told me I would close 2 weeks early.  In hindsight, I would take the hassle of digging out some extra docs for the lower rate with and straightforwardness of Better.com. The other mortgage broker showed his fee as $1000, gave me several disclosures showing $1000 to him?¦until the day of closing, then slipped in a new disclosure with a $4250 fee to him.   (This fee from Better would have been $500) So my choice was to cancel closing and lose my house that had a backup offer waiting, or close and pay him his illegal $4250. (They cannot legally increase fees within 5 days of closing).  I closed and paid it. And six months later - before I found time to report him for violating lending laws - the mortgage company realized it and sent me a check for $4250. 

So as a banker, I would recommend Better.com. And will use them if I ever buy another house.  Brokers can be sleazy and I feel that Better.com is a good company that follows the rules that are designed to protect you. They don?t have brokers. You pay them $500 straight up versus a broker getting paid $4250 for collecting your paperwork and emailing it to the lender. And it turns out, my mortgage would have closed on the same date no matter which lender I chose. They all set the date to what the contract said and made zero effort to close before that date. Although one lied and said we would close earlier and Better told the truth.

review_text:
Out of 7 lenders we chose Better

I'm meticulous about my own finances and wanted to make sure we went with a lender who would be easy to work with and help us make the best decision financially.  I found both at Better.  Our loan officer was easy to work with and was very responsive to our needs and questions.  We worked with them for a year since our new build kept being pushed back and I'm so glad we went with them.  Their policies are much more flexible than any other lender I could have found locally.  We are saving tons of money by closing a loan with them.  The only potential issue going with an out-of-state lender is maybe not having as close of hand-holding through the whole process.  However their portal is easy to use and as long as you get what they need quickly it's a very straightforward process.
One item Better could improve at is coordination between the client, loan officers, and underwriters. There were a few times where we'd tell one person one thing, but the actual change had to happen through another person and it didn't get done correctly.  However these were fixed by the end and were just a minor inconvenience.  I'd recommend Better to anyone.

review_text:
TL;DR: Better's loan service terms are very favorable to them and other lenders, but not ordinary people. Better misrepresents loan terms when pressed for details. Better is predatory, taking advantage of people's instinct to trust and ignorance of loan terms. Better rushes people to sign a loan agreement when individuals do not fully understand what they are agreeing to. 

Full Review: 

My family and I recently had a rather negative experience with them. The better agent I worked was great about making themselves available during the early part of the process, when there is not much to ask about. However, as we neared closing and needed to review documents, they were hard to get ahold of and were generally unknowledgeable about the terms of the contract. The agent I worked with frequently could not answer questions about the *124 page contract* we were expected to sign (and sent the day of the originally scheduled closing date). At one point, they indicated that since they were going to sell the loan, the original contract did not matter. This is not true. We found that if we had issues with any aspect of the contract, that simply meant we could not sign. There was zero opportunity to negotiate any aspect of the contract, regardless of how small. One of the terms indicated that if we were one day late of making a full payment, we could go into default - meaning the loan servicer could demand we pay the full loan amount at once (an impossible task for most people seeking a loan). So, if for some reason, there was a bank glitch, if I was unexpectedly in the hospital, or if I made a mistake and underpaid by $10, we could lose our home. There was no grace given in the contract. Better indicated over the phone it would take two fully missed payment to trigger that, but when pressed to put that interpretation into writing, they conceded that the original interpretation - one day late of any amount could trigger a default - was correct. 

There were additional loan terms that were likewise very risky to agree to. For example, 'any action' a borrower might be subject to that "in the judgement of the lender"puts the property at risk, you can be put in default. This language gives so much latitude to the lender to simply take your home and very little to the borrower. And they also wanted us to release our taxes so it could be shared and marketed. According to the better agents (who had already demonstrated themselves to be untrustworthy), I could opt out later, but that did not change that I would have to sign a contract agreeing to these terms. 

The better agents were like oil and silk on the phone, kindly trying to persuade me to do things I was not comfortable with, bending the truth, misrepresenting the facts and/or pleading ignorance of both the terms of the contract and U.S. law in order to get me to sign.  Zero out of the 5 stars, would never recommend.

I decided to experiment and try Better.com for the refinancing of our home.  It was a true delight!  The reason I say that is because I had an excellent liaison with Garrett Schirber.  Garrett always followed through, kept all his appointments and was deeply knowledgeable regarding any question I had.  He was encouraging and thoughtful. I am older (72) which means I have high expectations when needing help throughout the entire process.  Garrett met and exceeded all of my expectations.  
Garrett provided Outstanding Customer Service!

The other aspect that I liked was the incredible well designed, user-friendly online system.  It became easy to upload all required documents.  I tried to work with another lender to have an alternative offer but it was cumbersome and not so customer focused with timing.  This online solution is simple, direct and friendly to the non-tech user.  Great model. 
I am waiting now for the last step which is to have a new lender so we can begin the monthly mortgage payments!  
I have suggested Better.com to a few acquaintances and will continue to suggest it for home buyers. 
Thanks Garrett and Better for a smooth process.  
Melva Jo WDRondon (Colorado)

review_text: Horrendous experience. My rep repeatedly failed to return emails. When I called, he would not be available. When I set up a callback, his schedule was always full (for all times for months). When I left a message to call me, he'd sometimes not. And, most of all the valuation team required a roof replacement before funding the loan in W.Pa in January. My inspection said the roof was 20 yrs old, but sound. My agreement included escrow for roof replacement at closing. There was NO reason to require replacement before closing. Temp was consistently in the teens and 20s, but I needed to get a roofer to do the job now or no loan. My sales agreement said the roof replacement at escrow all along my process, but Better waited until the day before closing to say the roof MUST be replaced now. It delayed closing by 3 weeks. My seller had breast cancer getting radiation daily and lived in a hotel for those 3 weeks because she had emptied the house. There was no one who would listen to reason or logic. My rep said, "it's those other people." Our appraiser called them and said, "tell me where my appraisal says the roof is unsound and I'll take it out." Yet, they kept calling it "unliveable," when it simply was not. It is this kind of dangerous to enter into business with a company that has no logic, no reason, no integrity, and no empathy. BBB says Better gets 1.9 out of 5 for customer service, and you have to give a one - like here.

review_text: My wife and I were extremely pleased with our Better refinancing experience.  The process takes place totally online, with user-friendly webpages and prompts.  The company even sent a notary to our home so we wouldn't have to venture into COVID World to sign the closing documents.  Our Home Advisor, Johny Martinez, was respectful, knowledgeable, and professional in every way.  He seemed to be available around the clock via text to answer our questions, and was intimately familiar with the ins and outs of how the Better team does  mortgage refinancing.  My one disappointment was the prompts we received by email.  Some were a bit unclear (e.g., I got repeated prompts to e-sign a document, which I kept following puzzlingly to no avail until Johny explained that my wife had to sign, too.)  And some prompts weren't exactly...prompt.  For instance, the day after we closed on the loan we got a prompt saying we had five remaining tasks to complete before we could close.  All in all, however, our entire experience was exceptionally easy and flexible.  While Better is a business that's trying to make money just like every other lender, I felt that their entire process was geared to the borrower's interests, concerns, and needs.  So it's not hard for me to understand why so many consumers rate them so highly.

review_text:
I would like to express my concerns about the recent experience I had with Better Mortgage.   I have used Better Mortgage now for two transactions, a refinancing of my current home and a purchase of a new construction home.  The refinance was an incredibly easy and smooth experience and left me thinking how easy the online portal for Better Mortgage was to use. There was great communication throughout.  
As a result, I decided to go with Better Mortgage for a new construction home. I had already received a pre approval letter from the builders finance company but considered Better Mortgage based on my last experience. 
This to me now was a mistake. The experience was mostly disappointing.  
I want to first off commend Johnathon and Cole for their outstanding efforts and incredible communication throughout the process.  They were the best part of the experience.   Johnathon was always available to me and his communication via text and telephone was professional and decisive. 
 My complaints stem mostly with my experience with Annika and the cost of my loan.  

My interactions with Annika were informative and friendly.  The problem stems from the fact that they were inconsistent and that she was practically impossible to reach at times where I really needed to speak or text with her.  On the day before my loan closing, I had some concerns with the sudden change in closing costs and added fees to the loan.  We spend time going over this and she promised me she would look into it and let me know in the morning before closing.  When I didn?t hear from her, I called Better Mortgage and was told she was off work that day I was told to speak to Johnathon. A simple communication would have saved me time and effort in trying to reach out to her regarding a discrepancy on my loan.  Johnathon was great but he did not have any information on the previous evenings conversation with Annika and was put in a very precarious position.  I can only presume that Annika?s actions are based on any or all of the following:  that she is overworked, unorganized, not committed to a customer-oriented approach to her trade or simply unable to perform the most important part of this position which in my opinion is being available to the client and answering questions during a stressful period for most clients.  

The other major issue I had with this experience is the loan itself.  My initial loan disclosure as well as ALL subsequent loan disclosures had several credits outlined on them.  One of these credits was for over $4000.00.   This brought my cash to close to about 10k.   The day before I was closing this credit was removed and I was told that the builder refused to provide this credit.  Why was this not researched prior to this?  The process had been going on for several months and yet the team that was working on my loan researched every aspect of the loan and my qualification for the loan.  And yet they were unable to confirm this credit with the builder.   This to me seemed like a bait and switch tactic to make this loan appear more desirable than it really was.  The day of closing I had many questions about the loan that Annika had committed to answer and she was unavailable.  Johnathon was unable to answer to no fault of his own.  

I don?t need to go on about how I won?t be referring Better mortgage to other people and so on as we all know how social media and community recommendations are a valuable tool and selecting services.  However,  I would like some acknowledgment and or compensation from Better Mortgage to make things right.  I should never have been put in that position.  Buying a new home is supposed to be a positive experience, especially if it?s the first time.  My experience was completely tarnished as I had to scramble to find five thousand extra dollars the day of closing.  

I look forward to hearing from you regarding this disappointing experience. 

Moyez Charania. 
Loan Number : 1987541528

review_text: I was disappointed from the beginning. I was looking to buy around the border of two states.  The first realtor that called me failed to tell me that she was only licensed in one of those states so she was only sending me properties in the state she was licensed in and most of them were not even in the towns I told her I was interested in.  Which left me to do a lot of the leg work myself.  When I sent her a property I wanted to look at in the state she was not licensed in, she then partnered up with a realtor licensed in the other state. At first he made me feel like he cared about finding me the right home until it took longer than we thought.  He told me that he felt I was worried about how much of his time it took for him to come out and show me all the properties I saw and that I shouldn't worry about that. He obviously felt I took too much of his time.  Once I found a house and it came down to inspections, he recommended an inspector for me to use.  Never advised that I should get the well and septic inspected.  I asked about the well inspection only because a friend mentioned it to me and he replied "definitely good idea".  I also found it interesting that the only time the realtor was at a house before I arrived was the day of the inspection.  The inspector took a sample of the water and never let me know how the test came back, showed my pics of the well pipes when I was at the house but they are not in the final report I received. Missed a 14" long crack in one of the pipes in the crawl that was found after I moved into the house and my septic pump went out. The septic alarm was on silence mode, which I would think would be something an inspector would have noticed and mentioned. As for the HA I did not feel confident that he knew what he was doing.  They requested things from me that they had and I didn't due to the cash offer program I used (which was not explained to me in full detail).  I felt like they requested things at the last minute then made me feel like it was my fault I didn't get it to them in a timely manner. I was told one thing from the realtor and another from the HA.  My closing disclosure was sent to me incorrect multiple times. At one point my HA said I wasn't singing it I just needed to check the box that I received it and he would get me a corrected one ASAP.  I had to tell them what was missing or incorrect on the disclosure. I didn't receive a correct one until the night before my signing.  The title company contacted me to confirm my signing appointment and asked if I had my final disclosure and stated that they had not heard from my lender. I can keep going but what it boils down to is that I will not be recommending Better Mortgage or Better Real-estate to any of my friends or family.

review_text:
I have start with the fact that I eventually reached an impasse and did not refinance with Better.com.

The process started fairly positively, but they requested information about my company that does not belong to me (I am an officer and part owner). By their analysis they required detailed information about the company on a loan that was replacing one in good standing at a higher interest rate. They agreed to check with their underwriters about that request.

They followed that request with a completely over-reaching request for them to contact my company and ask for any information they wanted, including all of the information I had already refused to provide. When I balked at signing a form that was so broad reaching, they claimed to not be able to move forward without the company's tax returns and a profit and loss statement.

This information is known by only a handful of people, and I wasn't about to provide it to a third party whose privacy statement allows them to share it dozens of "affiliated" companies.

I was very disappointed since the ownership of the company was not new, and my current loan was provided without the company data. It was also requested in that case, but they understood that it was unreasonable to ask for data I do not own.

I still gave them 2 stars as I believe if I had not been honest about my ownership they would not have requested more information than, in my opinion, they are entitled the process would have been much smoother.

Nonetheless, I will not do business with, nor would I ever refer, better.com.

review_text: I started the process in November, but did not close until January. Expectations were a 30 day closing/refi. Better mortgage, a new player in the online mortgage industry, does have their act together virtually, a nice website, easy to navigate, automated tracking system... all work seamlessly as you go thru various steps of completeness and providing them with items they need to get your mortgage done.  And it may not be their fault, in today's economy with trying to deal with 3rd party vendors such as credit checking, title companies and appraisers; but, they are very hard pressed to get anything done in 30 days. Including 2nd and 3rd party closing teams... Most of the things being done 'outside' of their organization were agonizingly slow.  However, their in person (and online) points of contact were always very helpful, polite, and knowledgeable and kept me up to date regularly.   I give them 4 stars because they should do and be a little bit better on estimating loan closings.. know their markets a bit better if they're going to do nationwide lending.

Overall I would 100% recommend Better mortgage for getting your loan!

Imani was the wonderful representative who assisted me through my application and funding process.
Everything was great until the very end. I explained to Imani that stating to the average layperson that "funding" will take place on a certain day, leads the borrower to believe that the money will be made available that day.  So close to the Holiday and the end of year I was counting on the money to be there on "funding" day. When it did not appear to be direct deposited that day, I contacted Imani.  She explained that the money was first sent to a closing agent, who then would review it and then they would be responsible to direct deposit the money to my account.
I discussed with Imani that the process really should be explained in better detail.  Luckily the vendors I was working with understood my issue and all was good in the end. Some borrowers might not be that lucky.  It could easily cause problems.

Let me preface my comments by stating that these are our experiences, yours may differ.

Better.com was absolutely the best experience I have ever had with a mortgage (ours was a refinance).  It started out with a terrific rate, and then Mark became our loan advisor and things just kept getting better.  He explained what we needed to supply, why it was needed, and kept us appraised of everything that was taking place from the start of the approval process all the way through to the closing of our loan.  He, and Better, delivered exactly what they said they would, and did it in the time frame they said it would happen.  We didn't have to make multiple trips to a bank, we were able to do most of the paperwork online.  The title company sent a person to our home to finish the process (who does that).  Our loan was funded in less than 48 hours.  Again, I can't state it enough, Better.com and Mark and our closing agent far exceeded our wildest expectations.  I would absolutely recommend them to friends, family, and anyone else needing a mortgage loan or refinance.  Thanks to all of their staff for a really well done job.

review_text:
Better is convenient as you can do everything online, the team is responsive and the rates are attractive. However, it has a downside: there isn't much room for customization of approach, consideration of different circumstances, etc. It's very robotic: "that's the rule, that's how we do it". 
I also had some surprises on values during the process, which were corrected in the closing day, but only then. The taxes were over provisioned and my monthly payment was looking $500 more than what it actually was at the initial estimate, which made me uneasy. The closing costs also fluctuated a lot... I didn't have this same experience with other companies, it was stressful. 
All in all, everything went well, but it wasn't super smooth. 
One very good thing though: they were extremely helpful to get my verified pre-approval letter out the door. Worked very closely with me to expedite it, which I highly appreciated. 
Again, the team is responsive and polite, but limited, which makes their approach very robotic, not customized.

review_text: I think this is a good concept if you have no issues and don't need to interact with a qualified person.  It fits into our digital society and how we transact today.  In my case, I didn't close on a refinance I was seeking because Better.com changed the lender credits (money to offset closing costs) after the rate was locked (a $2,500 reduction in lender credits).  They claimed the change was due to a lower property value based on the appraisal.  They sent their contracted appraiser who came in with a value 10% lower than Redfin and 6% lower than Zillow.  The whole process felt like "bait-and switch" using a contrived property value to justify taking more profit for them.  Once this happened, trying to get a straight answer from someone was like trying to find a human to cancel an online subscription for something.   In the future, for something as complex as a real estate financing transaction, I think dealing with humans face to face is still the best route.  This isn't ready for prime time in my opinion.  Maybe some day.

review_text:
We wasted time and money working with Better.com and currently left in a limbo and finding our own way out. Writing this review to prevent more people getting stressed working with them.

These 1 star reviews are true!

For us, this is the worst experience we've ever had working on a loan!  Better.com's service is inefficient, inaccurate and incapable! The "technology" is just a buzz word for them. Please expect mistakes, unaccountable services, unattentative communications, delays and especially non-deliveries! This was honestly one of the worst experiences I have had. 
My representative did not explain things clearly along the way and just as i would have one letter finally sent from my bank (which was also like pulling teeth) another would expire and I would have to start again.
I had a charged off HELOC to deal with and when the bank that held the HELOC could not produce a letter with the required verbiage they requested my representative call them. He did so under protest and then was rude to them to the point that i had to apologize for his behavior when I next spoke to them.
Then to top it off just when it was all finally done (after TWO MONTHS) the numbers WERE NOT what we had discussed. My representative had completely disregarded the requests I had made weeks prior. It was cause for a panic attack.
This is the largest obligation in my life and to have the numbers be different in the last possible moment caused me to lose much faith in Better as a lender. 
In the end I told my representative to stop texting me and called into the 800 number and asked to speak to a manger. No one likes to be a Karen. :/ 
At this point, after strongly complaining, did I feel cared for enough to move forward with the loan ( but mostly it was because I didnt want to start somewhere new).
I would suggest better and more comprehensive training to your staff and perhaps with older customers like myself more phone calls would help over incessant texting which only compounded confusions.
In the end Better made it better but it should not have been bad to begin with.

review_text:
They had a great rate, then flags started popping up everywhere. We're super internet savvy, do nearly everything electronically, and have refinanced dozens of homes, so when we found Better on Nerd Wallet (shame on you Nerds - we used to trust you!), we thought Better was made for us. We were wrong. I'll try to list the issues we encountered clearly, from big to little:

1. Rate lock for 30 days, but then they become the bottleneck, and before you know it many thousands in extension fees are requested of you.
2. Look out for their disclosed network of companies. Part of their model is to immediately throw your business to a sister company to create fee income for themselves, often at inappropriate times/sequence. After looking, I realized their sister company fees for most things are higher than you can get elsewhere.
3. Very junior mortgage professionals. Our main person was decent, but then went on vacation and we got a total newb, who had no answers and escalated to his "team" which did absolutely nothing, repeatedly.
4. When leaving questions for "the team" online about certain docs, status, or process, I don't think we received a single definitive timely response or explanation. They clearly use an offshore back office team, and it doesn't work. This contributes to the Better Bottleneck.
5. When submitting one set of documents, this would spawn an entire new set of document requests, even ones that weren't applicable. This went on exponentially for the first two weeks. This, and the need to review (everything is "in review"), and answer questions also contributes to the Better Bottleneck.
6. During the process we finally had to arrange for closing. Then I found out that they don't do electronic closings. Our state has done pure electronic closings since 2008...but Better doesn't do them at all. 
7. The sequencing of actions, fees and referrals, lack of web-based capabilities (incl. the use of an old fashioned physical appraisal), poor service, and Better's Bottleneck, struck me as being manipulative and deliberate. Kind of like one might find in a New York or Jersey boiler room operation if it were being run by somebody that didn't know mortgage lending very well...and had even less familiarity with West coast mortgage lending. So I checked their location...guess what.

I know you're "sorry for my experience" folks, and that you'll "send my information to somebody who cares", but you won't change a thing. If this company goes public I would stay far away from it. I smell law suits coming.

review_text:
Better.com promises to provide a "not very competitive rate" in exchange for an easier experience with underwriting and processing the loan.
Unfortunately, while you DO get an uncompetitive rate, the process of getting a mortgage is still just as maddening and stupid as it ever was:
Explaining the same thing to an underwriter 3 times.  People quitting or getting reassigned in the middle of processing.
People going on vacation the day before closing, leaving a contact in their place that has also gone on vacation.

It's baffling and frustrating to have a 20% downpayment, excellent credit, and stand to make a lender 50% on the lifetime of a million dollar mortgage, and explain every nook and cranny of your finances ten times.  

I came expecting white glove service in exchange for the price, but I might as well have just gone to Bank of America.  Not a compelling service, and not recommended.

review_text: This was my first experience with Better and I am sadly disappointed.  I selected them for their great rates, online process, and the philosophy of no more frustrating mortgage experiences.  Better?s online process is generally pretty simple and steam-lined, but the process behind the scenes is a mess.  I had received pre-approval and locked in my rate in early August, expecting to close in mid-October.  ~ 7 weeks into the process, in late September, I am notified that they can not offer the loan to purchase the properties as outlined in my purchase agreement.  No one had read and understood the purchase agreement closely, even after I called attention to some uniquities in our agreement.  So we work through that by amendments to the purchase agreement, and it?s just been one thing after another since that.  6 revised purchase agreements later, 4 appraisal revisions, revising the loan structure twice, surprise decreases in promised lender credits, uncertainty on status of title search.  I am beginning to think they really wanted me to cancel?¦maybe they aren?t making enough off of my $500K loan.  I was scheduled to close on this loan tomorrow (third closing date because the first two couldn?t be met due to delays).  As of 6PM, I still have not obtained a copy of the final closing disclosure outlining settlement costs.  I?ve been asking for it all week since I?ve already experienced closing get rescheduled twice.  I was told by my loan agent that final balancing was underway (for 2 days), and then never updated again.  Needless to say, I am not going to closing at 9 am tomorrow because I am walking away from this lender.  The service was terrible and no one with authority was accountable to me as the customer throughout this process.  I got a bunch of apologies.  I did appreciate the positive attitudes of the people I dealt with though-out the process, they were very polite and apologetic, but this organization is poorly managed.  I am probably the profile for the ideal borrower - stable employment, high income, well documented cash and assets, near perfect credit report, etc. Their process is over-reliant on third-party affiliates whom they have no control over.  I would say that I pretty much drove the processing of this loan through the entire process. Was really willing to give this new company a try.  Started out great, but in the end I?ve spent too much of my own time to get through the process.  No call back on status at 12 hours before settlement is an absolute disgrace for customer service.

review_text:
Better.com promised a 1% rebate on the purchase price of my home if I worked with their referred realtor. I spoke to multiple people, multiple times that all promised this in writing and verbally. Guess what, more than a month after closing I still haven't received the rebate and no one will return my calls or emails. The last I heard was weeks ago and they were working on it. Not true! It's not worth the headache. I've spent hours trying to get them to deal with this and I can't get through to anyone. They are utterly incompetent but claim that they will always be available. A local loan officer will actually return your calls and keep their promises because they have to be accountable to everyone else in their local community, not like this giant corporation. 

Also, Alan Youngberg was my person and lied through his teeth throughout the whole process. Would never return my calls, emails, or texts. Wish I had walked away before it was too late.

The other people who made these promises included Lawrence Lee with Better Realty and Timothy Abogado/TJ Abogado.

The process of dealing with all of their "tasks" is a nightmare. It's all a poorly designed website made by someone who knows nothing about UI and seems to take pleasure in making things as frustrating as possible. Because it's a big corporation that is trying to streamline everything, it ends up taking way longer than necessary. You have no option to just send your documents to them or answer the questions directly. If the question doesn't apply to you, good luck getting them to take the "task" off your to do list and let you move to the next step.

On top of that, the majority of the people I spoke to literally understood less about mortgages than I, a layperson, did. Including someone (who I am feeling kind enough at this point to not name) who was supposed to answer my questions about something on my initial closing disclosure who kept insisting we should look at the loan estimate instead because it was easier to understand. When I tried to explain that made no sense because it was no longer up to date, my question had to do with the closing disclosure numbers, not the loan estimate numbers, and that I felt confident looking at the closing disclosure, he finally disclosed that HE did not know how to interpret it.

review_text:
This was my second refinance over the past couple of months. Refinancing my investment property didn?t go as smoothly as my residence. I first applied in July and had an appraisal done. The appraiser (who was ??new and unfamiliar? to the area) assessed my house at $151 a foot. I provided details as to why that was incorrect and my request to reevaluate the appraisal we denied. I asked for another appraisal to be done and was told the only way to have another appraisal was to go through the application process again. 

At no point was I told I would be subject to ??worst case? pricing or additional points if I go through another application. So I applied again hoping someone more reasonable would appraise my house. Someone with more knowledge or the area. I applied at the end of august and zero communication for weeks. I had to follow up with your team to find out when I was going to have the appraisal scheduled. I was told the appraisal company wasn?t reaching out Bc they saw I had a recent appraisal done but they were able to get the ball rolling again. So a month after I paid for the appraisal it was completed and viola!! They appraised it at $260,000, $24,000 more than the previous appraiser. 

So I?m disappointed in the communication or lack there of and I?m highly unsatisfied that I was penalized the way I was especially being a two time borrower who has nearly $600,000 in home loans with you all now. If the investment refi was my first experience I wouldnt have refinanced my primary residence. This is an offstage complaint. I am not taking this onstage because I would hope someone with authority will respond to my complaint.

review_text: I will say that this is my 2nd refinance with Better.  Unlike the first refi, this experience was stressful, unorganized, irritating, showed lack of attention to customer service, lack of communication skills and unprofessional as if I was dealing with people who did not know how to do their job.  Too many hands on the file and no communication amongst coworkers.  Repetitive and redundancy.  Closing was a nightmare had to sign 2x wrong information when it was explained to make sure there wouldn't be any mistake.  It was as if the questions asked and the answers given were outright ignored, thus when Notary arrived it was ALL wrong, but was told don't worry still sign call Better let them know. It will be ok.  So then had to resign a second time and only to find out a week later, only because I emailed Ashley asking when is funding taking place since it was to be 3 days after signing and then was told oh, you have tasks to do.  No auto emails were sent out letting me know when things were needed on a consistent basis.  Emails were unanswered for 2 to 3 days, until I complained and included manager did it get better but only to revert back to the same non-communication at closing.  I signed on September 27 and October 6.   Didn't get any calls emails or texts letting me know they needed the rescission docs yet again until I emailed Ashly on the 13th, because I'm getting notified and calls from Mr. Cooper my existing loan that I'm being charged a late fee and need to make payment by the 14th or incur  another 98.00 in  fees as well as Fremont Bank notifying me my payment is due.  She then sends me an email saying oh, we need you to sign the recission letter.  We can't fund unless we have that.  Well we all signed the recission letter 2x before and now 3 is a charm.  I told her my displeasure how I felt and she said Sorry, but at least we are at the end that's all we need and you will be funded on the 12th.  Well it was the 13th.  Shows how much attention to detail she had with my file.  So after that is done.  I get an email 10-14 a.m. stating FANNIE MAE guidelines needs and updated letter from your CPA to close we can't close unless the letter from your CPA is dated 10 days of the closing.  Well, had there not been all these mistakes or had someone known we had to sign yet a 2nd time they should have taken care of it then.  I am on vacation for my birthday and I have to deal with this, recontact my CPA to update the letter send it back as they tell me that this is the last thing they need to fund.  Well yet again another display of incompetency and such disorganization.  I get it done and receive an email hours later telling me congratulations your loan is funded.  Of course I go check and see and it is NOT.  I was told by Reilly Carter to go ahead and pay my mortgage to ensure that I don't get a negative  reporting on my Credit Report as that was my main concern.  I could go on and on with other issues throughout this process, but I would never refi with Better again.  This was so stressful, and I just felt like another number.  No personal touch did not feel like I was a value to your company but just another computer-generated file.  The left hand is definitely not letting the right hand know what they are doing as if everyone is running around with their heads cut off.  I would not refer Better to any of my colleagues or friends....   I really hope you all can get organized and perhaps train your staff how to communicate and fix the algorithms on your system to better streamline the process and notify clients when tasks are needed or you need additional information.  And definitely DON'T have clients sign closing docs when you have an incomplete file.  Signing the closing docs to a consumer means we are DONE.  I should NOT have to be running around signing rescission letters 3x having my CPA write and rewrite letters after the fact.   One dissatisfied customer's experience spreads faster than wildfire than 12 happy customers.

review_text: We had an absolutely wonderful experience with Better.com in our recent mortgage loan refinance in three very important and critical ways: (1) we locked in an historically low 2.5 percent interest rate on a 30-year mortgage loan with zero points; (2) the entire process, from initial loan application to closing took only about 18 calendar days; and (3) there were zero loan fees -- repeat, ZERO -- along with title costs that were incredibly low.  We're still wondering how Better.com made any money.  By contrast, last year we did a refinance with a regional loan company in which we paid over twice as much in title costs plus nearly $1200 in loan origination fees -- what a ripoff  compared to Better.com.  As for their loan processing and customer service, we also felt that they were exemplary: all of our borrower "tasks" were always clearly explained by their web-based system and then immediately processed.  Similarly, any questions we had (and we had quite a few) were quickly and courteously answered by email or phone calls.  Our experience is proof that Better.com is a shining example of Fintech at its finest.  Better.com really is better!!!

review_text:
I'm not normally one to write long reviews, but if I can save just one person from having an experience like mine, I have to in this case.

Better.com is the worst kind of digital company. They want all of the cost savings associated with being an online only originator, but don't deliver at all on the experience. Be prepared for ridiculous delays (we will get back to you within 24 hours claim is a joke), being forced to submit the same documentation over and over again with no explanation, and in the end having random decisions with no reasonable explanation.

I applied with high credit score and 15 year employment history with the same company. The mortgage amount was less than my annual income and I have more than enough cash in the bank to pay it off. For this low risk case, they somehow managed to drag out the process for four months. The low point for me was having to submit the same form for the fifth time after having it repeatedly rejected with no explanation (it was finally revealed that I had to check a box with a pen rather than typing an x on the computer). After uncountable similarly frustrating experiences, I thought I had finally emerged from their torturous process when I got my approval notification and a task to schedule my closing. How wrong I was! They follow this up with a text saying that they will now not move forward with my loan. Their stated reason: I travel too much for work. I don't even know how to respond to that.

review_text:
Update: I was able to get a formal letter of loan denial from better after writing this review. Hilariously, the reasons listed for my denial are not any of the ones I listed below. They seem to be picked at random (Too many delinquent accounts? I have no delinquent accounts) so I guess the bots at Better just randomly selected me to get turned down. Sweet.

--

This is a sham business with Kafka-esque communication and customer service process. Do NOT be taken in by their low interest rates and tech-savvy appearance. I was preapproved for a mortgage by Better on 7/19/21. At multiple times in this process, I asked my contact, Julia, if I would be approved considering I have an unconventional income and have been denied loans in the past due to this. She assured me it would not be a problem. (In retrospect I believe Julia had no professional knowledge and never really knew what she was talking about, nor did any of the dozens of minions I was rerouted to whenever I tried to contact my loan consultant.) With that information I booked travel to my desired location. I was given ANOTHER pre-approval letter on 8/19/21 to reflect the house's cost. I put in an offer on 8/19/21 and it was accepted 8/20/21.

On 9/20/21, one month later, after putting down earnest money, paying for inspections and insurance, booking another trip for closing, expediting my divorce (really) to confirm I had no financial liability in my separation, and paying a $550 "rate locking fee" I was told I had been denied. The reasons I was given could have been detected in the pre-approval process, which is why I asked over and over again at the top of this process. Worse, they determined that the apartment I rent negatively affected my debt-to-income ratio. I DISCLOSED MY RENT IN THE PRE-APPROVAL PROCESS. My loan consultant Jonathan denied this. Later, he took it back, confirming that I had indeed disclosed my rent. (LOL.) They assumed I wouldn't be paying it anymore. Why would you assume that? It was as if nobody had looked at my financials before then.

Worse, I was instantly locked out of my account and can't contact anyone there or get any documentation of my application and denial. My emails and calls go unanswered (my calls are often just dropped.) I have no recourse but to leave reviews like this and hope nobody else gives this rickety tin can of a company their business. (I also suspect many of these positive reviews, which sound suspiciously familiar, are plants.)

review_text: Started process with them on May 4th, supposedly cleared title 2 weeks later.  Claim was that they were "looking for an appraiser" since my property had some acreage attached and "only certain people were able to appraise these types".  Got several follow-up emails asking about the parcels and their descriptions, literally asking the exact same questions.  They couldn't wrap their brains around the fact that the mortgage covered more than one parcel.  At some point during this, I got an email saying they had to look for another appraiser because "I never responded to messages from the appraiser." This was a complete lie, confirmed.  After 2 months of waiting, I finally called a supervisor, who told me in fact that the appraisal wasn't ordered in May, but at the END OF JULY because of their incompetence dealing with the two parcels.  So after 4 months of waiting, lots of back and forth, and lots of lies, I get an email saying they are closing my application because they "can't find anyone willing to appraise the property" when I know for a fact the company who appraised it less than 2 years ago is still operating and not very far from the property (about an hour away), not uncommon for rural properties.  AVOID THESE LIARS LIKE THE PLAGUE.  And to think the email said, please consider us in the future.  I've never had a team of more incompetent amateurs all the way up to the supervisors in my life...  yeah, right.

review_text:
I used to work in the mortgage industry so I have a pretty good idea of how this process works. They get 2 stars for having the lowest rates and for fast processing and underwriting times. The rest of the experience was a bit of a mess and clunky. So you start with putting in what type of loan you're trying to qualify for then it asks you for the normal documents. I was doing a refinance and rolling in the closing costs so I wouldn't need any cash to close. It didn't correctly add in the amounts at the start of the loan and then also asked me for bank statements to show sufficient funds to close. Once again, rolling in costs so don't need to provide that. Had to reach out to processor to adjust both issues. Went through processing and UW very quickly as I had sent them everything they needed quickly. Moved onto closing, what a mess. First closing disclosure (CD) was correct so we set up signing. Then as signing approached CD was changed and all of a sudden I needed to bring around $1,000 cash to close. Emailed and messaged closer to correct. No correspondence until after my signing. I was left messaging my notary during the day saying we might not actually be signing because the docs were wrong. So had to cancel 1st signing. For the next week we played try to correct the CD. I found out, yes in fact they made a mistake on the CD. What followed was around 8 different CDs. Good luck trying to keep up with all of those and the changes and which one is most recent, because their system is poorly set up to track that. It took a week to finally get it corrected and set up new signing. When the notary came, there were 2 different CDs again including an incorrect one. Finally got everything taken care of and the loan funded. They transferred it to The Money Source (TMS) to service. Had an awful customer experience with them, where their VP of Customer Experience had to get involved and told me that their customer care team had sent me the wrong information.  Now my loan servicing is being transferred on the day my 1st payment is due on a holiday weekend, so we'll see how that goes. Hopefully as it moves further away from Better.com my experience will get better.

Update on the servicing being transferred: Why in the world would Better.com think it is a good idea to transfer servicing on the day the 1st payment is due!?!? I'm 11 days into the month now and have not been able to make my payment, I've tried online and over the phone and it will not work. Who thought it would be a good idea to have this be the timing of the service transfer? I can not stress enough how much I do not recommend working with Better.com. I've had mortgages with Wells Fargo and US Bank in the past and never had this much trouble. I am now subtracting the 1 extra star I gave for rates and speed due to the servicing fiasco.

review_text: Five stars (rounding from about 93%). First, here's what I found smooth and solid: everything from entry to the transition from loan preparation to closing. Your website is very user-friendly in design, layout, navigation, content, feedback to user, and access to support. I really loved the educational materials. Also, the processes I encountered on my account page on the website were tightly linked to personnel who were highly accessible, and provided timely, thorough, and clear explanations and guidance. That made my experience of the loan application, analysis, approval, and preparation processes really seamless and low-stress. I'm a newbie at finance! However, I felt like we hit a wall when we got to the loan closure process, insofar as I felt like I was working blind and alone. I didn't know what to expect. So there was a lapse in support there, that I initially attributed as a personnel issue. However it may also (or entirely) have been structural; the information guiding me through the loan process during the closing steps were nowhere near as granular as they had been during other steps (e.g., official emails from BMC tying to notices on my account page; or even the time-line on my account page). Now that we're in the funding phase, I feel like I'm back in business with the BMC whose praises I've been singing to friends and family again. I'm getting an email from someone every day or so, explaining what's happening, and what to expect in the path ahead. What a relief! I hope these remarks have been clear. I don't actually know the names of the various phases of the loan process, so I've tried to guess which terms would make these smooth (mostly) and bumpy (closing) waypoints clear. Thanks for what has been, overall, a great experience. Cheers, Robin

Smooth sailing for a straightforward loan.  Very easy experience from application to closing.  All of the associates I worked with via email and phone were courteous and professional, efficiently providing me with the necessary help needed.  Loan was approved very quickly.  Even my realtor commented multiple times on how smooth the transaction was.  While my experience was very good, I can see why one size does not fit all: 
1. The amount of documentation required for a mortgage can seem overwhelming for some, and having to do it all yourself online without immediate feedback can cause issues.  Also, my loan application was very clean avoiding many scenarios that may not be optimally solved without face to face interaction.
2. Better.com associates are extremely overloaded.  Emails took days to answer, and sometimes calendars for booking a phone call appeared completely booked.  
3. Multiple points of contact during the process.

Will not hesitate to use Better.com for any future mortgage needs.

review_text:
Generally speaking, love the platform that Better has built to simplify the byzantine process of qualifiying for a mortgage as well as meeting the reporting requirements.  The engine up front for estimating differnt loan configurations and automatically generating prequal letter was the best I saw during the shopping process.  Also the process of receiving "tasks" from the loan team was straightforward.  

Only a few suggestions.  One...would be nice to have a better idea of what paperwork is required further on in the process, in many cases it would have been easier just to tell me up front what you'd need so I can compile it all at once.  Second, I'm sure you understand that making the out of pocket for down payment and closing costs is a mad dash to generate liquidity (ie cash).  So...if I'm going to have to sell a car, or ask a relative for a gift, or even do an early withdrawal from my retirement, you should let me know up front what the paperwork is that you'll need (e.g., take a picture of that car title!).  Finally, the video course on buying a home was REALLY helpful, but should be assigned to the borrower as a task much earlier on.  

However, overall it was a good though stressful experience, and would recommend you to others.

review_text:
Overall, Better did an absolutely fantastic job handling our loan. Our processing expert, Micheal Squilante, was readily available and knowledgeable. He answered my questions and emails in a timely fashion and worked diligently on our behalf to close ON TIME, which I couldn't appreciate more.

There were two items that made the process give me a little anxiety. 

#1. Our home was being built in a rural area where the county and the city did not do a certificate of occupancy or a municipal inspection report. While our processing expert offered many items we could produce in lieu of this, there was no clear, defined documents outlined as possibilities. I don't think the underwriting team told him exactly what we needed to offer so we collected as much random permit/inspection information as possible and submitted it. It would have made me feel a lot better had we been given a clear list of items that would have worked instead as it was all left up to hoping what we did provide would be good enough. That made me feel as if there were a lot of unknowns at the end and the possibility of our file being returned by underwriting at the last minute was not ideal considering all the planning we had to do for moving.

#2. I'll preface this by saying that our closing expert handled everything quickly, and he clearly knew what he was doing. It all came down to the wire in the end, but it did get handled and there were no mistakes. However, the communication at this point was lacking. There were several emails not replied to and I had to call to get updates. When it's the last few days before closing, this is not ideal and caused no small amount of anxiety about having to change movers, installers, ect around. While he did a fantastic job getting the closing over the finish line, I felt like my emails were going into the abyss and it left me feeling unsure about whether or not things were going to happen as scheduled. 

As I said, overall the experience was a great one and Better gave me a great rate, great closing costs and the web portal was incredibly easy to use. Not sure if banks do something similar these days, but being able to upload everything was quick and easy and being able to easily check my loan estimate made this whole thing much less stressful than emailing documents back and forth wondering if I've provided all that was needed.

review_text: Better.com provided me a loan for the purchase of a property in Houston, TX. I selected them as the lender for the following reasons: 1. Lowest Rate, 2. Offered a Jumbo Loan with a 11% down payment, whereas most other institutions  required a 20% downpayment, 3. No PMI, 4. Low fees. The negative aspects of the process are as following: 1. You won't be dealing with a dedicated person throughout the process. Depending on the stage of the lending process, you'll be connected to different people, with different responsibilities. The problem with that is that you have to be constantly repeating yourself when calling to resolve any particular "issue" you're dealing with. Another problem that I experienced was that early on I was given incorrect information about the credit history requirements for the loan, and I thought that I would not be able to qualify because I virtually have zero debt. Only after discussing the issue with another employee, I realized that the underwriting requirements were misunderstood by the original loan officer. Overall, it was worth to use Better.com because of the financial conditions of the loan, but it demanded a bit of patience and persistence from me.

Working with Better was straightforward for the most part but communication and professionalism could be improved. I did not appreciate that I would receive an email notifying me of changes in my loan estimate, that was always resulted in an increased amount, without an explanation of the reason for the change. 

My initial estimated home value was 15% less than the appraised value, and I was refused an alteration in the rate or points available based upon this new information. However, my closing costs kept increasing based upon new information that I was required to provide. It made me feel taken advantage of, and that the $500 I spent for the required home appraisal was wasted and unused.

When I would contact my Processing Expert, Lauren San Miguel, I did not receive legitimate reasons for questions I had regarding these issues. Additionally, she did not return my emails or calls for over two weeks. Due to the delays, I was within a day of my rate lock expiration when my loan was finally funded. Only after I started working with Karly McCabe did I receive responses expected with a professional financing company.

review_text:
I have financed and refinanced mortgages with the same lender for over a decade, so I was initially a bit apprehensive in starting the process with Better.com.  However, my concerns quickly faded within the first couple of days.  I began the refinancing process through Better.com on a Friday, and I was immediately contacted via email and/or phone call by the team members that had been assigned to me so that they could introduce themselves.  All questions that I had over the weekend while getting my documentation together and deciding on a rate lock were answered immediately.  In fact, my loan consultant, Scott actually called me on that Sunday shortly after I had emailed him with a question.  Everything was explained and mapped out for me in detail by Scott.  The same can be said for Brie and Charlotte, my loan processing experts, and Dylan, my closing expert.  Both were very responsive and took the time to answer any questions that I had.  Brie even went as far as to call me one evening just to check in to make sure that I didn't have any unanswered questions.  

In total my refinance took three weeks from start to close.  Considering all that's involved in a refinance, I was very impressed by the efficiency in the Better.com process.  The user portal was very easy to navigate, and uploading necessary documentation so that the team could approve my loan was simple.  Overall, I am extremely happy with my refinancing through Better.com, and I will definitely be referring family and friends.

review_text: This is the 2nd time we refinanced with Better Mortgage (1st happened quick and easy in summer of 2020). We found Better on nerdwallet where it's ranked A+. 1st mortgage refi with Better was a breeze and done in 3 weeks (it took one week to find a notary public willing to visit a remote area). If we'd asked for a notary in town it'd be a 15 day refi, literally. It's hard to believe it was a breeze as I assess our financial situation as complicated involving more paperwork than a straightforward refi. The 2nd refi was a challenge and it took 3 tries to make it happen. 1st refi try went nowhere because it was discovered that there was a million dollar lawsuit against our HOA, and no bank would touch that. 2nd refi attempt, the lawsuit was thrown out but we had to abort the mission due to a family disagreement (California's brute handling of the virus been tough on folks in the gold state). Better understood no questions asked.  The 3rd time's the charm refi just finally happened, but took over 2 months. The reason was something else... Our HELOC bank has 1 star [out of 5] rating and it was slowing us down with their 21 day response policies (our original HELOC was sold to that horror bank). The nightmare bank's HELOC "mortgage" had to be what's called subordinated to the new refi loan (duckduckgo what loan subordination is). Better finally got what we wanted out of the hell bank.  Better are the STELLAR amongst the good the bad and the ugly. They go above and beyond. We've locked into the lowest rate set to expire April 5, '21 yet the rate was honored until April 17 when we signed. I've seen what an old school refi is with the use of fax and "you must get" A to get B, but you can't get A unless you already got B mumbo-jumbo and reps spacing out when you got to leave messages to the guy and his manager and their director to make things budge. Better Mortgage took the nonsense and the runaround out of refi. Now's the time to refi- because negative interest rates are VERY unlikely in US. And just think of it- if that was to happen YOU'd pay a bank some interest just for keeping your $ in the bank! I don't think there'll be better time to refi as you'll get the same thing next year at best- so why stagnate with paying your ok bank more for longer??? Aim at getting done with mortgage for good. The myth that you'll save a ton on taxes writing interest off is just a myth. Think how much you'll save when you pay your place off and there's no more paying to the big brother??? Picture this: No more mortgage payments! Go for shorter term- you save a ton!  Worst thing that will happen you will create a bunch of online accounts to download forms, or just install a free document scanner app and scan away those 20-40 documents or less they'll ask all in all and next thing you know- the rifi is done! You'll look at your new mortgage statement and see how fat principal rapidly goes skinny! Don't sit on it! Don't skip a beat and Better.com won't skip one! Go for it! If you do like 1040EZ taxes (or close to that) all mighty himself wants you to refi like yesterday as it's EZ quick, all painless, and simple!  ;) And here's one cute detail for ya: I did not mind taking an hour out of my vacation to sign papers on Hawaii - was done at a mall dining area during CoViD. Yep, Better will make it happen for you if you don't mind. My vacation got a whole lot better because we'd signed on the Big Island :)

review_text:
Strung Along and then denied
My experience with this internet based mortgage company was very unsatisfactory. After several weeks of supplying an endless list of documents for a refinance of a single family private residence and being self employed, my loan application was denied. The denial was simply on the basis that my business (lodging) lost money due to Covid state travel restrictions in 2020 after over 20 years of generating significant annual net income over $100,000. My credit, loan to value, payment history, assets (over several million) were all very strong as they acknowledged. The extremely disappointing aspect of this interaction is that they did not tell me that I would not qualify up front which would have saved considerable wasted effort on my part. Their approach to assigning tasks for obtaining information at first appeared efficient and doable except after completing the first batch of tasks, more were assigned. The task process seemed to go on endlessly with no end in sight. As I said, a simple inperson interview could have avoided all the wasted effort on my part. They operate very decentralized with a very impersonal approach. I definitely would not recommend dealing with Better Mortgage as in my experience they did not live up in any way to their company name.

Please note:  Comments on this company are under several different listings.

review_text:
It's been a few years since we refinanced, and this was the first time we used an "automation assisted" mortgage company to refinance our home. review_text:
I had the worst experience I ever had refinancing a mortgage with Better.com.  First the whole automated site may work unless you actually need to talk to anyone.  They constantly ping you with "tasks" many times asking for the same information.  I didn't find any of the actual people I worked with knowledgeable and they have the most inept and incompetent underwriting department you would have the displeasure of working with.

But the main issue was how they qualify RSU income.   First they told me they don't qualify RSU as income.  I complained and fought with them and they didn't budge. Then I found an actual page on their own site saying they do qualify the income! Once I sent them their own page they relented and qualified the income.

The biggest joke came when they described how they qualified.  They used the least amount of stock vested in a 3 year period and randomly chose a stock price anywhere from the previous 2 years. Anyone working in a tech company knows they typically grant RSU's yearly that vest in a 4 year cycle which means in year 3 your stocks would be at or near the lowest level because that's your furthest vest date.  Long story short they ended up qualifying .18% of my total RSU grant!  That's not a typo.

Please stay far away from this company and find a company that's actually qualified to do their job.
